slides.title: Executive summary
slides.body:
Executive Comparison:  
The Access Review Evidence Log addresses gaps in vendor control evidence for Q1 2024, requiring full provenance, ownership, and archival of artifacts to close controls, while the Privileged Account Approval Email governs temporary, scoped access for specific personnel under strict compliance and oversight controls. The former resolves missing evidence to enable control closure; the latter enfors conditional access with quarterly reviews and vendor validation.

- Access Review Evidence Log requires archival of email-export artifacts to resolve outstanding evidence (EV-002).  
- Privileged Account Approval Email mandates vendor confirmation of personnel for standing privileged access.  
- Evidence completeness is a prerequisite for control closure in the Access Review Log.  
- Privileged access is granted conditionally under scoped, logged, and quarterly-reviewed terms.  
- The Access Review Log focuses on vendor control evidence gaps; the email addresses personnel access governance.  
- Archiving artifacts resolves status gaps; quarterly recertification enforces ongoing compliance.
